name: Waterfall — Standard
description: Pre-populated starter content for traditional sequential project delivery with defined phases, milestone-based governance, and fixed-scope contractual structure.

executiveSummary.content:
This engagement delivers {{customer_name}}'s {{project_name}} initiative using a structured Waterfall delivery model. The project follows a clearly defined sequence of phases — Requirements, Architecture & Design, Build, Testing, and Deployment — with formal milestone approvals between each phase ensuring full alignment before progression.

The Waterfall approach is appropriate for this engagement given the well-understood requirements, stable technology landscape, and {{customer_name}}'s governance framework requiring milestone-level sign-off. Deliverables are defined upfront in the Statement of Work, with a formal change control process governing any scope variations.

inScope.text: Requirements elicitation and documentation (Functional & Non-Functional Requirements)
Solution architecture and detailed technical design
Development and unit testing of all agreed components
System Integration Testing (SIT) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) facilitation
Project closure report and lessons learned documentation

outOfScope.text: Scope changes after baseline Requirements sign-off (subject to formal Change Request process)
Ongoing operations and support beyond the stabilisation period
Business process re-engineering not directly related to the technical solution

waterfallApproach.deliveryApproach:
The project will be executed in five sequential phases with milestone gate reviews between each:

1. Requirements (Weeks 1–3): Structured workshops with {{customer_name}} business and technical stakeholders. Output: signed Functional & Non-Functional Requirements document.

2. Architecture & Design (Weeks 4–6): Solution architecture, component design, interface specifications, and security design. Output: Technical Design Document (TDD), signed at Design Gate.

3. Build (Weeks 7–14): Development and unit testing by the Microsoft delivery team. Build progress tracked weekly against the agreed Work Breakdown Structure (WBS).

4. Testing (Weeks 15–18): SIT (Microsoft-led) followed by UAT ({{customer_name}}-led). Defects managed in Azure DevOps with P1/P2 blocker criteria for go-live approval.

5. Deployment (Week 19–20): Production cutover per the agreed Cutover Plan; 2-week stabilisation support period.
waterfallApproach.supportTransitionPlan: 2-week post-deployment stabilisation: Microsoft resources available at 50% for critical issue resolution. Formal handover at end of stabilisation including as-built documentation, runbook, and support escalation contacts.

phasesMilestones.successCriteria: All functional and non-functional requirements documented and signed by {{customer_name}} sponsor
Technical Design Document approved; architecture security review passed
All components developed; unit test pass rate ≥98%; code review approved
UAT pass rate ≥95%; zero open Critical/High defects; {{customer_name}} sign-off
Production deployment completed; cutover checklist signed off by both parties
Stabilisation period complete; all deliverables accepted; closure report signed

deliverables.title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements Document
deliverables.description: Complete, baselined requirements document signed off by {{customer_name}}

deliverables.title: Technical Design Document (TDD)
deliverables.description: Architecture diagrams, component specifications, interface contracts, security design

deliverables.title: SIT Completion Report
deliverables.description: SIT test results with defect register and resolution evidence

deliverables.title: UAT Completion Sign-Off
deliverables.description: UAT results signed by {{customer_name}} stakeholders confirming acceptance

deliverables.title: Deployed Production Solution
deliverables.description: Fully configured and tested solution deployed to {{customer_name}} production environment

deliverables.title: Runbook & Operations Guide
deliverables.description: Operational runbook covering deployment, rollback, monitoring, and escalation

deliverables.title: Project Closure Report
deliverables.description: Project summary, lessons learned, and formal acceptance sign-off

assumptions.text: Requirements can be fully defined and baselined prior to the Architecture & Design phase
{{customer_name}} will assign a Business Analyst or subject matter expert to participate in requirements workshops
All infrastructure and environments (development, test, production) will be provisioned by {{customer_name}} before the Build phase begins
Milestone sign-off decisions will be provided within 5 business days of deliverable submission

customerResponsibilities.text: Provide SMEs and business stakeholders for requirements workshops and milestone reviews
Provision and manage environments (development, test, production) and required access
Lead UAT execution, track defects, and provide sign-off within agreed timelines
Manage internal change management and end-user communications

risks.text: Requirement changes after baseline — mitigated by formal Change Request process with documented impact assessment
Environment delays impacting Build phase — mitigated by environment readiness checkpoint at Design Gate
UAT resource constraints — mitigated by scheduling UAT participants and test windows at project kickoff

members.responsibilities: Executive governance, milestone approval, commercial oversight
Schedule, RAID log, stakeholder communications, milestone tracking
Requirements elicitation, FRD authoring, UAT facilitation
Architecture design, TDD authorship, technical governance
Lead development, code reviews, build governance
SIT planning, test execution, defect management, UAT support

teamStructure.supportTransitionPlan: 2-week stabilisation support at 50% allocation. Formal handover session including runbook walkthrough, escalation path, and transition to {{customer_name}} support team.
